task: I need to send a short update to a few people about a work issue that’s taking longer than expected, and I want the message to be easy to follow without adding extra back-and-forth. Can you help me draft it so it sounds professional and fits a normal work update?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, professional update you can send:

**Subject:** Update on [Issue/Project Name]

Hi all,  
Quick update: this is taking longer than expected to resolve. I’m still working through it and will share another update by [time/date] or as soon as I have more to report. Thanks for your patience.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it:
- more formal,
- more friendly,
- or tailored to a specific issue/project.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close. I’d want it a little firmer and less vague — like, say what’s delayed, what’s being done, and when the next update is coming. Normal work update, not too apologetic.

Maybe something like:

Hi all,  
Quick update: this issue is still in progress and is taking longer than expected to resolve. I’m working through the remaining steps now and will send another update by [time/date] or sooner if I have something concrete to share. I’ll keep this moving and let you know as soon as there’s a clear next step.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you can, make it a little tighter and more direct than the first version.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, more direct version:

Hi all,  
Quick update: this issue is still in progress and is taking longer than expected to resolve. I’m working through the remaining steps now and will send the next update by [time/date], or sooner if I have something concrete to share. I’ll keep this moving and will update you as soon as there’s a clear next step.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it:
- a bit firmer,
- more concise,
- or tailored to a specific audience like leadership or a broader team.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically right. I’d just want it a little less wordy and a bit firmer — cut the repeated “update” language and make the delay/next step clearer.

Something like:

Hi all,
Quick update: this issue is still in progress and is taking longer than expected because [brief reason / step]. I’m working through the remaining items now and will send the next update by [time/date].

Best,
[Your Name]

If you want, make it even tighter for leadership or a broader group.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, firmer version:

Hi all,  
Quick update: this issue is still in progress and is taking longer than expected due to [brief reason / remaining step]. I’m working through the remaining items now and will send the next update by [time/date].

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it:
- a bit more polished,
- more executive/leadership-friendly,
- or even shorter for a broad group.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Tight enough, firm enough, and not overexplaining it.
